judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports hit all five expectations with correct citations and near-identical substance; they are comparable.

report_1.evidence: shows failures began at 14:03 and the first alert fired at 14:50 — a 47-minute detection gap — with on-call acknowledgment at 15:05 and mitigation at 15:20

report_2.evidence: The timeline shows failures began at 14:03 and the first alert fired at 14:50 — 47 minutes later. "Our team responded immediately" is also misleading: on-call acknowledged at 15:05, over an hour after failures started. Evidence: `incidents/OPS-412-timeline.md`

report_1.evidence: `larkpay-comms/incidents/OPS-412-timeline.md` records that 1,284 transactions failed and required manual reprocessing, and 37 were unrecoverable and refunded

report_2.evidence: The internal timeline (`incidents/OPS-412-timeline.md`) records that 1,284 transactions failed and required manual reprocessing, and 37 were unrecoverable and refunded

report_1.evidence: `larkpay-comms/docs/sla-commitments.md` states the contractual monthly SLA is 99.9% and any month below it is a breach owing service credits scaled to the depth of the breach. `larkpay-comms/metrics/uptime-2026.csv` shows April 2026 at 99.77% — a breach.

report_2.evidence: April 2026 uptime was 99.77% (`metrics/uptime-2026.csv`), below the 99.9% contractual monthly SLA in `docs/sla-commitments.md`, which states any such month "is a breach and owes service credits."

report_1.evidence: Remove the blame on the on-call engineer.** "The outage was caused by the on-call engineer deploying an untested config change" violates rule 1 of `larkpay-comms/docs/customer-comms-style-guide.md` ("Never assign blame to an individual").

report_2.evidence: The draft blames a named individual role, violating style guide rule 1.** Draft lines 12–13 attribute the outage to "the on-call engineer deploying an untested config change." `docs/customer-comms-style-guide.md` rule 1: "Never assign blame to an individual." This must be rewritten

report_1.evidence: Restructure to lead with customer impact and remediation.** The draft opens with a reputation defense and buries the (single-sentence) remediation at the end, violating rule 3 of `larkpay-comms/docs/customer-comms-style-guide.md`. It should open with what happened to customers

report_2.evidence: The draft does not lead with customer impact and remediation, violating style guide rule 3.** It opens with an uptime boast... Per `docs/customer-comms-style-guide.md` rule 3, the structure must be inverted: impact and remediation first
